items.headline: One sleep to the final — and there's a France-England consolation game today
items.body: The World Cup ends tomorrow: Messi's Argentina vs. Spain, 3pm ET at MetLife, with Argentina chasing an unheard-of four straight major titles and Spain riding a defense that's conceded exactly once all tournament. Today's the appetizer — France and England, the two beaten semifinalists, meet in the third-place playoff in Miami this evening.
items.say: World Cup final's tomorrow — Messi's Argentina against Spain. There's even a France-England game today for third place. Who you got in the final?

links.text: ESPN · World Cup today: France-England 3rd place, final looming
ESPN · The final — Argentina vs. Spain, what you need to know

items.headline: A THIRD 62 in two days — Ryan Fox joins the record book and grabs a share of the lead
items.body: Royal Birkdale has turned into a birdie carnival. Ryan Fox poured in nine birdies for a 62 on Saturday — the eighth score of 62 in major-championship history and, incredibly, the third in just 48 hours after Lucas Herbert and Sam Burns did it Friday. Fox is tied with Herbert at 8-under going into Sunday, with Scottie Scheffler lurking three back.
items.say: Golf's gone nuts at The Open — three guys have now shot 62, ties the major record, and two of them did it back-to-back days. Ryan Fox is tied for the lead going into Sunday. Scheffler's right there too.

links.text: PGA Tour · Round 3 live updates & leaderboard
Golf Monthly · Ryan Fox on the charge at Royal Birkdale

items.headline: Teenager on pole at Spa — Kimi Antonelli beats Verstappen for a shock front row
items.body: Qualifying at F1's favorite track delivered a jolt: 19-year-old Kimi Antonelli charged to pole at Spa, edging Max Verstappen by three-tenths for his sixth pole of the season. There's a twist for the grid, too — Lando Norris set the third-fastest time but drops to 13th with an engine-parts penalty, bumping George Russell up to start third. Race is Sunday, 9am ET.
items.say: Wild F1 qualifying at Spa — the 19-year-old kid Antonelli grabbed pole ahead of Verstappen. Race is Sunday morning, and it's the best track of the year. Worth setting the alarm.

links.text: Formula 1 · Antonelli charges to pole in exhilarating qualifying
The Race · Belgian GP qualifying results

items.headline: Pogačar just keeps winning — a solo Stage 14 in the Vosges stretches his lead past four minutes
items.body: Back in the mountains, Tadej Pogačar attacked with 7km to go, hauled in the breakaway and rode away alone for his fourth stage win of this Tour. His lead over Jonas Vingegaard is now a commanding 4:30. The feel-good story of the day was 19-year-old Frenchman Paul Seixas, who climbed all the way to fourth overall. Easy lazy-Saturday background watch.
items.say: Pogačar won again at the Tour — solo in the mountains, and he's over four minutes clear now. Kind of running away with it. Perfect thing to have on in the background this afternoon.

items.headline: Baseball's back — Dodgers edge the Yankees 2-1 in a World Series rematch, Ohtani returns
items.body: The second half opened Friday in the Bronx with a Fall Classic flavor, and the Dodgers took it 2-1. Max Muncy's two-run homer off Gerrit Cole in the seventh was the whole ballgame, Roki Sasaki turned in a strong start, and Shohei Ohtani was back in the lineup as DH after getting his knee drained over the break. He's slated to pitch again Wednesday.
items.say: Baseball's back — Dodgers beat the Yankees 2-1 last night, Muncy took Gerrit Cole deep for the only runs. Ohtani's back in the lineup too. You following the pennant races?

items.headline: Nolan's 'The Odyssey' is a monster — $51M on Friday alone, the biggest opening day of 2026
items.body: The numbers are in and they're huge. Christopher Nolan's IMAX epic, with Matt Damon as Odysseus, banked $51 million on Friday — the best single day of any movie this year — and is now tracking toward a $117M+ opening weekend. That one Friday already out-grossed the entire opening weekend of Nolan's 'Interstellar.' The 'see it on the biggest screen' crowd was right.
items.say: That new Nolan movie, The Odyssey, made $51 million on Friday alone — biggest opening day of the whole year. Everyone's saying see it in IMAX. You catching it this weekend?

links.text: Variety · 'The Odyssey' makes $51M on opening day

items.headline: Nerd Christmas is almost here — San Diego Comic-Con kicks off Wednesday
items.body: The biggest pop-culture gathering of the year is nearly upon us, running July 22–26. Marvel is returning to the legendary Hall H stage ahead of 'Avengers: Doomsday,' the live-action 'Avatar: Aang' movie will preview its cast, and Prime Video is bringing 'Rings of Power' Season 3 — so expect a week of trailer drops and jaw-on-the-floor first looks to talk about.
items.say: Comic-Con starts Wednesday — Marvel's back in Hall H for Avengers: Doomsday, and there's a live-action Avatar movie coming. Basically a whole week of trailer drops. You geek out over any of that stuff?

links.text: Deadline · Comic-Con 2026 schedule of movie & TV panels

items.headline: The ESPYs handed out sports' biggest trophies — A'ja Wilson and Jalen Brunson take top athlete
items.body: Sports' answer to the Oscars went down this week in New York, hosted by comedian Marcello Hernández. A'ja Wilson won Best Athlete in women's sports and Knicks star Jalen Brunson took the men's award, while Steph Curry picked up the Muhammad Ali humanitarian honor and figure skater Alysa Liu was named Best Breakthrough Athlete. A fun, feel-good night of speeches and highlight reels.
items.say: The ESPYs were this week — A'ja Wilson and Jalen Brunson won top athlete, and Steph Curry got the humanitarian award. It's basically the Oscars for sports. Always some good speeches.

links.text: ESPN · Full list of 2026 ESPY winners
